name: Grafana MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Grafana MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Grafana — querying dashboards, exploring data sources, checking alerts, querying metrics/logs via Prometheus and Loki, and investigating incidents.
use_cases: Querying Grafana dashboards for current metric state in agent workflows
Checking active alerts and alert history from incident response agents
Exploring Prometheus metrics and Loki logs via Grafana data sources
Agent-driven incident investigation using Grafana observability stack
Generating on-demand metric summaries for status reporting agents
not_for: Teams not using Grafana for observability
Direct Prometheus or Loki API access (use those APIs directly instead)
Grafana dashboard creation and modification (read-heavy use case)
best_when: An agent is performing incident investigation, on-call triage, or automated observability queries against a Grafana-backed monitoring stack.
avoid_when: You need to create or modify dashboards programmatically — Grafana's HTTP API directly is better for that.

auth.notes: Service Account tokens recommended for agent use — supports fine-grained roles (Viewer, Editor, Admin per organization). API keys legacy but still supported. Basic auth discouraged.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Grafana OSS is free and open source; Grafana Cloud free tier: 10K active series, 50GB logs
pricing.paid_tiers: Grafana Cloud Pro: ~$8/month; Enterprise: custom with SLA and advanced features
pricing.notes: Grafana OSS self-hosted is free. Grafana Cloud has a generous free tier. MCP server is open source.

performance.rate_limits: Grafana Cloud: rate limits vary by data source and tier

known_agent_gotchas: Data source query complexity varies — PromQL/LogQL required for metric/log queries
Dashboard panel data requires parsing JSON model to extract meaningful queries
Grafana organizations are separate tenants — service account is org-scoped
Alert evaluation state vs notification policy are different concepts — agents must understand both
Large metric queries can be slow — agents should use time range limits
Self-hosted Grafana needs URL configuration — no default cloud endpoint
agent_readiness.security_notes: Service Account tokens with Viewer role recommended for read-only agents. HTTPS enforced on cloud. Role-based access at org level. Audit logs in Enterprise.

security_score_components.security_notes: Service account tokens with role-based access provide good security posture. HTTPS enforced on cloud. Self-hosted requires manual TLS. SOC 2 Type II for cloud.
